heading: National Multimodal Freight Network
label: 70103 National Multimodal Freight Network
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 70103 was transferred and renumbered as , National and Commercial Space Programs.section 50903 of Title 51Prior sections 70104 to 70121 were transferred and renumbered as sections 50904 to 50923 of Title 51, National and Commercial Space Programs.

children.text: to assist States in strategically directing resources toward improved system performance for the efficient movement of freight on the Network;
to inform freight transportation planning;
to assist in the prioritization of Federal investment; and

to assess and support Federal investments to achieve the national multimodal freight policy goals described in and the national highway freight program goals described in .section 70101(b) of this titlesection 167 of title 23

children.heading: Designation of National Multimodal Freight Network.—
children.label: b Designation of National Multimodal Freight Network.—
A
children.text: improving network and intermodal connectivity; and

children.label: B
children.text: using measurable data as part of the assessment of the significance of freight movement, including the consideration of points of origin, destinations, and linking components of domestic and international supply chains.

children.label: A
children.text: origins and destinations of freight movement within, to, and from the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: volume, value, tonnage, and the strategic importance of freight;

children.label: C
children.text: access to border crossings, airports, seaports, and pipelines;

children.label: D
children.text: economic factors, including balance of trade;

children.label: E
children.text: access to major areas for manufacturing, agriculture, or natural resources;

children.label: F
children.text: access to energy exploration, development, installation, and production areas;

children.label: G
children.text: intermodal links and intersections that promote connectivity;

children.label: H
children.text: freight choke points and other impediments contributing to significant measurable congestion, delay in freight movement, or inefficient modal connections;

children.label: I
children.text: impacts on all freight transportation modes and modes that share significant freight infrastructure;

children.label: J
children.text: facilities and transportation corridors identified by a multi-State coalition, a State, a State freight advisory committee, or a metropolitan planning organization, using national or local data, as having critical freight importance to the region;

children.label: K
children.text: major distribution centers, inland intermodal facilities, and first- and last-mile facilities; and

children.label: L
children.text: the significance of goods movement, including consideration of global and domestic supply chains.

children.label: A
children.text: use, to the extent practicable, measurable data to assess the significance of goods movement, including the consideration of points of origin, destinations, and linking components of the United States global and domestic supply chains;

children.label: i
children.text: the factors described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: any changes in the economy that affect freight transportation network demand; and

children.label: C
children.text: provide to the States an opportunity to submit proposed designations from the States in accordance with paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: consider nominations for additional designations from metropolitan planning organizations and State freight advisory committees, as applicable, within the State;

children.label: ii
children.text: consider nominations for additional designations from owners and operators of port, rail, pipeline, and airport facilities; and

children.label: iii
children.text: ensure that additional designations are consistent with the State transportation improvement program or freight plan.

children.heading: .—Critical rural freight facilities and corridors
children.label: B .—Critical rural freight facilities and corridors
i
children.text: is a rural principal arterial;

children.label: ii
children.text: provides access or service to energy exploration, development, installation, or production areas;

children.label: I
children.text: a grain elevator;

children.label: II
children.text: an agricultural facility;

children.label: III
children.text: a mining facility;

children.label: IV
children.text: a forestry facility; or

children.label: V
children.text: an intermodal facility;

children.label: iv
children.text: connects to an international port of entry;

children.label: v
children.text: provides access to a significant air, rail, water, or other freight facility in the State; or

children.label: vi
children.text: has been determined by the State to be vital to improving the efficient movement of freight of importance to the economy of the State.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A State may propose additional designations to the National Multimodal Freight Network in the State in an amount that is not more than 30 percent of the total mileage designated by the Under Secretary in the State.

children.heading: .—Determination by under secretary
children.label: ii .—Determination by under secretary
children.text: The Under Secretary shall determine how to apply the limitation under clause (i) to the components of the National Multimodal Freight Network.

children.label: i
children.text: a list of any additional designations proposed to be added under this paragraph; and

children.label: I
children.text: the State has satisfied the requirements of subparagraph (A); and

children.label: II
children.text: the designations referred to in clause (i) address the factors for designation described in this subsection.

children.heading: .—Condition for acceptance
children.label: E .—Condition for acceptance
children.text: The Secretary shall accept from a State a designation under subparagraph (D) only if the Secretary determines that the designation meets the applicable requirements of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Redesignation of National Multimodal Freight Network
children.label: c .—Redesignation of National Multimodal Freight Network
children.text: Not later than 5 years after the initial designation under subsection (b), and every 5 years thereafter, the Assistant Secretary, using the designation factors described in subsection (b), shall redesignate the National Multimodal Freight Network.
